ogTitle: St Leonards vs Staffordshire Reef: Climate Safety

heading: St Leonards vs Staffordshire Reef — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between St Leonards, Victoria and Staffordshire Reef, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: St Leonards has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Staffordshire Reef (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is St Leonards or Staffordshire Reef safer from flooding?
faq.answer: St Leonards has a flood risk score of 48/100, while Staffordshire Reef scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: St Leonards scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Staffordshire Reef scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in St Leonards or Staffordshire Reef?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. St Leonards has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.73% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
